Package org.rodinp.core.basis

Class InternalElement

java.lang.Object
org.eclipse.core.runtime.PlatformObject
org.rodinp.core.basis.RodinElement
org.rodinp.core.basis.InternalElement
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable, IElementManipulation, IInternalElement, IParent, IRodinElement, ISnapshotable
Direct Known Subclasses:
ComponentRoot, Data, Dependency, EventBElement, EventBProofElement, ImplicitHolder, Language, NamedElement, NamedElement2, PRExprRef, PRPredRef, PRReasoner, PRStringInput, Reference, RefinesEvent, RodinTestDependency, RodinTestRoot, RodinTestRoot, RodinTestRoot2, SCInternalContext, UbiquitousElement, VersionEA, VersionEB, VersionEC, VersionRootA, VersionRootB, VersionRootC, VersionRootD, VersionRootE, VersionRootF, VersionRootG, VersionRootH
public abstract class InternalElement extends RodinElement implements IInternalElement
This abstract class is intended to be implemented by clients that contribute to the org.rodinp.core.internalElementTypes extension point.

This abstract class should not be used in any other way than subclassing it in database extensions. In particular, database clients must not use it, but rather use its associated interface IInternalElement.

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • IInternalElement

  • Constructor Details

    • InternalElement

      public InternalElement(String name, IRodinElement parent)

  • Method Details

    • clear

      public void clear(boolean force, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IElementManipulation
      Removes all attributes and children of this element, forcing if specified and necessary.
      Specified by:
      clear in interface IElementManipulation
      Parameters:
      force - a flag controlling whether underlying resources that are not in sync with the local file system will be tolerated (same as the force flag in IResource operations).
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element could not be cleared. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource (CORE_EXCEPTION)
      • This element is read-only (READ_ONLY)

    • copy

      public void copy(IRodinElement container, IRodinElement sibling, String rename, boolean replace, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IElementManipulation
      Copies this element to the given container.
      Specified by:
      copy in interface IElementManipulation
      Parameters:
      container - the destination container
      sibling - the sibling element before which the copy should be inserted, or null if the copy should be inserted as the last child of the container
      rename - the new name for the element, or null if the copy retains the name of this element
      replace - true if any existing child in the container with the target name should be replaced, and false to throw an exception in the event of a name collision
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element could not be copied. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element, container element, or sibling does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource
      • The container is of an incompatible type (INVALID_DESTINATION)
      • The sibling is not a child of the given container (INVALID_SIBLING)
      • The new name is invalid (INVALID_NAME)
      • A child in the container already exists with the same name (NAME_COLLISION) and replace has been specified as false
      • The container or this element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • The destination element is a root element (ROOT_ELEMENT)

    • create

      public void create(IInternalElement nextSibling, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Creates this internal element in the database. As a side effect, all ancestors of this element are open if they were not already.
      Specified by:
      create in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      nextSibling - sibling before which this element should be created (must have the same parent as this element), or null to create this element in the last position
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if the element could not be created. Reasons include:
      • The parent of this element or the given sibling does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • The parent of this element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • There already exists a child element with the given type and name (NAME_COLLISION)
      • The given sibling is invalid (INVALID_SIBLING)
      • A CoreException occurred while accessing an underlying resource
      See Also:
      • IInternalElement.createChild(IInternalElementType, IInternalElement, IProgressMonitor)

    • createChild

      public final <T extends IInternalElement> T createChild(IInternalElementType<T> type, IInternalElement nextSibling, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Creates a new child of this element in the database and returns a handle to it. As a side effect, this element and all its ancestors are open if they were not already. The name of the created child is automatically assigned by the database to prevent any name collision.
      Specified by:
      createChild in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      type - type of the child to create
      nextSibling - sibling before which the child should be created (must have this element as parent), or null to create the child in the last position
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if the child could not be created. Reasons include:
      • This element or the given sibling does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • This element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • The given sibling is invalid (INVALID_SIBLING)
      • The given type is not allowed for a child of this element (INVALID_CHILD_TYPE)
      • A CoreException occurred while accessing an underlying resource
      See Also:
      • IInternalElement.create(IInternalElement, IProgressMonitor)

    • createElementInfo

      public InternalElementInfo createElementInfo()

    • createProblemMarker

      public void createProblemMarker(IAttributeType.String attributeType, int charStart, int charEnd, IRodinProblem problem, Object... args) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Creates a new Rodin Problem marker for the given String attribute of this element, and located at the given position in the attribute.

      The new marker is attached to the underlying resource of this element. Its marker type is RodinMarkerUtil.RODIN_PROBLEM_MARKER.

      Specified by:
      createProblemMarker in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      attributeType - type of the attribute to mark, or null if no attribute should be marked
      charStart - start position (zero-relative and inclusive), or a negative value to indicate its absence
      charEnd - end position (zero-relative and exclusive), or a negative value to indicate its absence
      problem - problem to attach to the new marker
      args - arguments to the problem
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this method fails. Reasons include:
      • This element does not exist.
      • The specified attribute is not set for this element, although start and end positions have been specified.
      • The start and end positions are specified together with a null attribute id.
      • The start and end positions are specified with an attribute id whose kind is not string.
      • The start or end position is negative, but not both.
      • The end position is less than or equal to the start position.
      See Also:
      • RodinMarkerUtil

    • createProblemMarker

      public void createProblemMarker(IAttributeType attributeType, IRodinProblem problem, Object... args) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Creates a new Rodin Problem marker for the given attribute of this element.

      The new marker is attached to the underlying resource of this element. Its marker type is RodinMarkerUtil.RODIN_PROBLEM_MARKER.

      Specified by:
      createProblemMarker in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      attributeType - type of the attribute to mark, or null if no attribute should be marked
      problem - problem to attach to the new marker
      args - arguments to the problem
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this method fails. Reasons include:
      • This element does not exist.
      See Also:
      • RodinMarkerUtil

    • delete

      public void delete(boolean force, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IElementManipulation
      Deletes this element, forcing if specified and necessary.
      Specified by:
      delete in interface IElementManipulation
      Parameters:
      force - a flag controlling whether underlying resources that are not in sync with the local file system will be tolerated (same as the force flag in IResource operations).
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element could not be deleted. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource (CORE_EXCEPTION)
      • This element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • This element is a root element (ROOT_ELEMENT)

    • exists

      public boolean exists()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns whether this Rodin element exists in the database.

      Rodin elements are handle objects that may or may not be backed by an actual element. Rodin elements that are backed by an actual element are said to "exist", and this method returns true. For Rodin elements, it is always the case that if the element exists, then its parent also exists (provided it has one). Moreover, for any element which is not a stable file snapshot, the parent includes the element as one of its children. It is therefore possible to navigate to any existing Rodin element (except stable file snapshots) from the root of the Rodin database along a chain of existing Rodin elements. On the other hand, a stable snapshot file never shows up among the children of its parent element.

      The fact that an openable element exists gives no guarantee upon whether it can be opened. For instance, a malformed Rodin file may exist, while it can never be opened by the database (because it contains invalid XML).

      A call to this method might open the ancestors of this element, but will never open this element itself.

      Specified by:
      exists in interface IRodinElement
      Specified by:
      exists in class RodinElement
      Returns:
      true if this element exists in the Rodin database, and false if this element does not exist

    • getAttributeTypes

      public IAttributeType[] getAttributeTypes() throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Returns an array of the types of all attributes currently attached to this element. If this element doesn't carry any attribute, an empty array is returned.

      The file containing this element is opened by this operation.

      Specified by:
      getAttributeTypes in interface IInternalElement
      Returns:
      the types of all attributes of this element
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource

    • getAttributeValues

      public final IAttributeValue[] getAttributeValues() throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Returns an array of the values of all attributes currently attached to this element. If this element doesn't carry any attribute, an empty array is returned.

      The file containing this element is opened by this operation.

      Specified by:
      getAttributeValues in interface IInternalElement
      Returns:
      the values of all attributes of this element
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource

      public final IAttributeValue getAttributeValue(IAttributeType attrType) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Returns the value of the attribute with the given type and carried by this element.

      The file containing this element is opened by this operation.

      Specified by:
      getAttributeValue in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      attrType - type of the attribute
      Returns:
      the value of the attribute with the given type
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element or the specified attribute does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource

      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Returns the value of the attribute with the given long integer type and carried by this element.

      The file containing this element is opened by this operation.

      Returns:

      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Returns the value of the attribute with the given string type and carried by this element.

      The file containing this element is opened by this operation.

      Returns:

      Throws:

    • getCorrespondingResource

      public org.eclipse.core.resources.IResource getCorrespondingResource()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the resource that corresponds directly to this element, or null if there is no resource that corresponds to this element.

      For example, the corresponding resource for an IRodinFile is its underlying IFile. The corresponding resource for an IRodinProject is its underlying IProject. Internal elements have no corresponding resource.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getCorrespondingResource in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the corresponding resource, or null if none

    • getElementInfo

      public InternalElementInfo getElementInfo(org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from class: RodinElement
      Returns the info for this handle. If this element is not already open, it and all of its parents are opened. Does not return null.
      Specified by:
      getElementInfo in class RodinElement
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if the element is not present or not accessible

    • getElementName

      public final String getElementName()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the name of this element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getElementName in interface IRodinElement
      Specified by:
      getElementName in class RodinElement
      Returns:
      the element name
      See Also:
      • IRodinElement

    • getElementType

      public abstract IInternalElementType<? extends IInternalElement> getElementType()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the type of this element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getElementType in interface IInternalElement
      Specified by:
      getElementType in interface IRodinElement
      Specified by:
      getElementType in class RodinElement
      Returns:
      the element type
      See Also:
      • IRodinElement

    • getInternalElement

      public <T extends IInternalElement> T getInternalElement(IInternalElementType<T> childType, String childName)
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Returns a handle to a child internal element with the given type and name.

      Since Rodin 3.0, the database enforces restrictions on the type of child elements. The given type must be allowed as a child type for the type of this element through extension point org.rodinp.core.itemRelations.

      This is a handle-only method. The child element may or may not be present.

      Specified by:
      getInternalElement in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      childType - type of the child element
      childName - name of the child element
      Returns:
      the child internal element with the given type and name
      See Also:
      • IInternalElement.createChild(IInternalElementType, IInternalElement, IProgressMonitor)
      • IInternalElementType.canParent(IInternalElementType)

    • getMutableCopy

      public final InternalElement getMutableCopy()
      Description copied from interface: ISnapshotable
      Returns a handle to this element in the mutable copy of its Rodin file.

      This is a handle-only method. The element may or may not be present.

      Specified by:
      getMutableCopy in interface IInternalElement
      Specified by:
      getMutableCopy in interface ISnapshotable
      Returns:
      this element in the mutable copy of its Rodin file

    • getNextSibling

      public final IInternalElement getNextSibling() throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Returns the internal element that follows this element in the children list of its parent or null if this element is the last sibling.

      This method is useful when one needs to create an element just after another. It allows to get the parameter to be passed to the create() for properly ordering the created element.

      Specified by:
      getNextSibling in interface IInternalElement
      Returns:
      the next sibling of this internal element or null if this element is the last child of its parent
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource

    • getOpenable

      public RodinFile getOpenable()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the first openable parent. If this element is openable, the element itself is returned. Returns null if this element doesn't have an openable parent.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getOpenable in interface IInternalElement
      Specified by:
      getOpenable in interface IRodinElement
      Specified by:
      getOpenable in class RodinElement
      Returns:
      the first openable parent or null if this element doesn't have an openable parent.

    • getPath

      public org.eclipse.core.runtime.IPath getPath()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the path to the innermost resource enclosing this element. The path returned is the full, absolute path to the underlying resource, relative to the workspace.

      This is a handle-only method.

      This is a convenience method fully equivalent to getOpenable().getFullPath().

      Specified by:
      getPath in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the path to the innermost resource enclosing this element

    • getResource

      public org.eclipse.core.resources.IFile getResource()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the innermost resource enclosing this element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getResource in interface IInternalElement
      Specified by:
      getResource in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the innermost resource enclosing this element

    • getRodinFile

      public RodinFile getRodinFile()
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Returns the Rodin file containing this internal element.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getRodinFile in interface IInternalElement
      Returns:
      the Rodin file containing this element

    • getRoot

      public final InternalElement getRoot()
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Returns the root ancestor of this element. If this element is a root element, it is returned. Otherwise, the nearest ancestor which is a root element is returned.

      For an element belonging to a Rodin file, this is fully equivalent to 

      getRodinFile().getRoot()

      Specified by:
      getRoot in interface IInternalElement
      Returns:
      the root ancestor of this element
      See Also:
      • IRodinElement.isRoot()

    • getSnapshot

      public final InternalElement getSnapshot()
      Description copied from interface: ISnapshotable
      Returns a handle to this element in the snapshot of its Rodin file.

      This is a handle-only method. The element may or may not be present.

      Specified by:
      getSnapshot in interface IInternalElement
      Specified by:
      getSnapshot in interface ISnapshotable
      Returns:
      this element in the snapshot of its Rodin file

    • getSimilarElement

      public final IInternalElement getSimilarElement(IRodinFile newFile)
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Returns a handle to the element which has the same relative path as this element, but relative to the given file. In particular, if this element is a file, then the given file is returned.

      Since Rodin 3.0, the database enforces restrictions on the type of child elements as declared through extension point org.rodinp.core.itemRelations. If this element is not a root element and if the root element type of the given file does not allow as child the type of the below root ancestor of this element, then an IllegalArgumentException is raised, as the handle to the similar element cannot be built.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getSimilarElement in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      newFile - file in which to construct the new handle
      Returns:
      an element with the same path relative to the given file as this element

    • getUnderlyingResource

      public org.eclipse.core.resources.IFile getUnderlyingResource()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns the smallest underlying resource that contains this element, or null if this element is not contained in a resource.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      getUnderlyingResource in interface IInternalElement
      Specified by:
      getUnderlyingResource in interface IRodinElement
      Returns:
      the underlying resource, or null if none

    • hasAttribute

      public boolean hasAttribute(IAttributeType type) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Tells whether this element carries an attribute with the given type.

      The file containing this element is opened by this operation.

      Specified by:
      hasAttribute in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      type - type of the attribute
      Returns:
      true iff this element carries an attribute with the given type
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource

    • hasChildren

      public boolean hasChildren() throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IParent
      Returns whether this element may have one or more immediate children. This is a convenience method, and may be more efficient than testing whether getChildren is an empty array.

      If this method returns false, one can be sure that this element doesn't have any child. On the contrary, if true is returned, this means that the answer is not known (this element may or may not have children).

      Specified by:
      hasChildren in interface IParent
      Specified by:
      hasChildren in class RodinElement
      Returns:
      true if this element may have immediate children, false otherwise
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource

    • hasSameAttributes

      public boolean hasSameAttributes(IInternalElement other) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Returns whether this element and the given element have the same attributes in the Rodin database. Two elements have the same attributes if and only if:
      • they both don't exist or they both exist and
      • they carry the same attributes (if any) with the same values.

      Specified by:
      hasSameAttributes in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      other - the element to test for similar attributes
      Returns:
      true iff this element and the given element have the same attributes in the Rodin database
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an error was encountered while comparing the elements

    • hasSameChildren

      public boolean hasSameChildren(IInternalElement other) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Returns whether this element and the given element have the same children in the Rodin database. Two elements have the same children if and only if:
      • they both don't exist or they both exist and
      • their children (if any, and taken in order) have the same contents

      Specified by:
      hasSameChildren in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      other - the element to test for similar children
      Returns:
      true iff this element and the given element contain the same children in the Rodin database
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an error was encountered while comparing the elements or their descendants.

    • hasSameContents

      public boolean hasSameContents(IInternalElement other) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Returns whether this element and the given element have the same contents in the Rodin database. Two elements have the same contents if and only if:
      • they have the same element name and element type.
      • they both don't exist or they both exist and:
        • they carry the same attributes (if any) with the same values;
        • their children (if any, and taken in order) have the same contents.

      Specified by:
      hasSameContents in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      other - the element to test for similar contents
      Returns:
      true iff this element and the given element contain the same subtree in the Rodin database
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if an error was encountered while comparing the elements or their descendants.

    • isReadOnly

      public boolean isReadOnly()
      Description copied from interface: IRodinElement
      Returns whether this Rodin element is read-only. An element can be read-only for two reasons: either it corresponds to a resource which is read-only, or it is an internal element that belongs to a stable snapshot of a Rodin file, or the stable snapshot itself.

      This is a handle-only method.

      Specified by:
      isReadOnly in interface IRodinElement
      Overrides:
      isReadOnly in class RodinElement
      Returns:
      true iff this element is read-only

    • isSnapshot

      public final boolean isSnapshot()
      Description copied from interface: ISnapshotable
      Returns whether this is a handle in a file snapshot.

      This is a handle-only method. The element may or may not be present.

      Specified by:
      isSnapshot in interface ISnapshotable
      Returns:
      true iff the corresponding element is or belongs to the stable snapshot of a Rodin file

    • move

      public void move(IRodinElement container, IRodinElement sibling, String rename, boolean replace, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IElementManipulation
      Moves this element to the given container.
      Specified by:
      move in interface IElementManipulation
      Parameters:
      container - the destination container
      sibling - the sibling element before which the element should be inserted, or null if the element should be inserted as the last child of the container
      rename - the new name for the element, or null if the element retains its name
      replace - true if any existing child in the container with the target name should be replaced, and false to throw an exception in the event of a name collision
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element could not be moved. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element, container element, or sibling does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource
      • The container is of an incompatible type (INVALID_DESTINATION)
      • The sibling is not a child of the given container (INVALID_SIBLING)
      • The new name is invalid (INVALID_NAME)
      • A child in the container already exists with the same name (NAME_COLLISION) and replace has been specified as false
      • The container or this element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • This element or the destination element is a root element (ROOT_ELEMENT)

    • removeAttribute

      public void removeAttribute(IAttributeType attrType, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Removes the attribute with the given type from this element. If the attribute didn't exist previously, this method returns directly, no exception is thrown.

      The file containing this element is opened by this operation.

      Specified by:
      removeAttribute in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      attrType - type of the attribute to remove
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource

    • rename

      public void rename(String newName, boolean replace, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IElementManipulation
      Renames this element to the given name.
      Specified by:
      rename in interface IElementManipulation
      Parameters:
      newName - the new name for the element
      replace - true if any existing element with the target name should be replaced, and false to throw an exception in the event of a name collision
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element could not be renamed. Reasons include:
      • This Rodin element does not exist (ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST)
      • A CoreException occurred while updating an underlying resource
      • The new name is invalid (INVALID_NAME)
      • A child in the container already exists with the same name (NAME_COLLISION) and replace has been specified as false
      • This element is read-only (READ_ONLY)
      • This element is a root element (ROOT_ELEMENT)

    • setAttributeValue

      public void setAttributeValue(IAttributeType.Boolean attrType, boolean newValue, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Sets the value of the attribute with the given boolean type to the given value. If the specified attribute didn't exist, it is created.

      The file containing this element is opened by this operation.

      Specified by:
      setAttributeValue in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      attrType - type of the attribute
      newValue - value to set
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource or if this element cannot carry an attribute of the given type
      See Also:
      • IInternalElementType.canCarry(IAttributeType)

    • setAttributeValue

      public final void setAttributeValue(IAttributeType.Handle attrType, IRodinElement newValue, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Sets the value of the attribute with the given handle type to the given value. If the specified attribute didn't exist, it is created.

      The file containing this element is opened by this operation.

      Specified by:
      setAttributeValue in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      attrType - type of the attribute
      newValue - value to set
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource or if this element cannot carry an attribute of the given type
      See Also:
      • IInternalElementType.canCarry(IAttributeType)

    • setAttributeValue

      public final void setAttributeValue(IAttributeType.Integer attrType, int newValue, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Sets the value of the attribute with the given integer type to the given value. If the specified attribute didn't exist, it is created.

      The file containing this element is opened by this operation.

      Specified by:
      setAttributeValue in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      attrType - type of the attribute
      newValue - value to set
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource or if this element cannot carry an attribute of the given type
      See Also:
      • IInternalElementType.canCarry(IAttributeType)

    • setAttributeValue

      public final void setAttributeValue(IAttributeType.Long attrType, long newValue, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Sets the value of the attribute with the given long integer type to the given value. If the specified attribute didn't exist, it is created.

      The file containing this element is opened by this operation.

      Specified by:
      setAttributeValue in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      attrType - type of the attribute
      newValue - value to set
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource or if this element cannot carry an attribute of the given type
      See Also:
      • IInternalElementType.canCarry(IAttributeType)

    • setAttributeValue

      public final void setAttributeValue(IAttributeType.String attrType, String newValue, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Sets the value of the attribute with the given string type to the given value. If the specified attribute didn't exist, it is created.

      The file containing this element is opened by this operation.

      Specified by:
      setAttributeValue in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      attrType - type of the attribute
      newValue - value to set
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource or if this element cannot carry an attribute of the given type
      See Also:
      • IInternalElementType.canCarry(IAttributeType)

    • setAttributeValue

      public void setAttributeValue(IAttributeValue newValue, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Description copied from interface: IInternalElement
      Sets the given attribute value to this element. If the specified attribute didn't exist, it is created.

      The file containing this element is opened by this operation.

      Specified by:
      setAttributeValue in interface IInternalElement
      Parameters:
      newValue - value to set
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if this element does not exist or if an exception occurs while accessing its corresponding resource or if this element cannot carry an attribute with the given value
      See Also:
      • IInternalElementType.canCarry(IAttributeType)

    • toStringInfo

      public InternalElementInfo toStringInfo(int tab, StringBuilder buffer)
      Description copied from class: RodinElement
      Debugging purposes
      Specified by:
      toStringInfo in class RodinElement
